sutta: monks possessing 5 qualities 1 who waits on the sick is not fit to help the sick what 5 he cannot prepare medicines number 2 he does not know what is beneficial from what is unbeneficial offers what is not beneficial does not offer what is beneficial thirdly in hope of gain he waits on the sick not out of goodwill foughtly he loads to to move to remove excrement or urine or vomit hospital fifth nor can he from time to time instruct raoul's gladden and satisfy the sick with dharma talk monks possessing these 5 qualities 1 who waits on the sick is not fit to help the sick that's the end of the suta
commentary: here is talking about the person who is not suitable to tend to a sick person the first 1 he does not know how to prepare medicines the second 1 he does not know what is beneficial for the sick person or what is not beneficial for the sick person the third 1 he has ulterior motive when he tends on when he tends to the sick person he's not doing it out of goodwill but he's trying to get something out of it the fourth 1 he he does not like to remove excrement or urine or vomit hospital he can't stand these things and then the last 1 he does not how to give a beneficial talk [Music] he does not know how to talk encouraging talk beneficial talk to the sick person these are the 5 qualities which makes a person not qualified not to look after a sick person the next utah is

items: does not know how to prepare medicines
does not know what is beneficial or what is unbeneficial
has ulterior motive when tending to the sick
does not like to remove excrement, urine, or vomit hospital
does not know how to give a beneficial talk
